arguments.task: Research Discord using official first-party pages only where possible. Open Discord developer docs, auth docs, rate limits docs, support/legal/developer policy docs, pricing/access pages if relevant, and GitHub/MCP registry pages if official or clearly tied to Discord. Extract evidence for: one-line product description; API auth methods supported for developers/bots/apps; whether access is self-serve or gated and why; API surface type (REST, GraphQL, Both, None) and breadth; existence of an MCP server/integration if any official or notable public repo exists; rate limit notes; notable signals such as gateway/webhooks/slash commands/app directory/SDKs. Favor official docs and first-party pages over blogs. Return a structured summary with exact supporting URLs. If a page is inaccessible, use another official Discord page. Do not use community posts unless no first-party source exists for MCP evidence.

input.thought: Launch browser research against official Discord sources and a narrow MCP check.

raw_result.description: Communication platform with APIs for bots, apps, webhooks, OAuth2 integrations, and realtime gateway events.
raw_result.auth_notes: Discord developer docs describe OAuth2 for user authorization and bot authorization, plus bot tokens used to authenticate bot/API requests. No evidence gathered for API keys or basic auth.
raw_result.gating_reason: Developers can create apps and use the public API through self-serve developer docs, but some capabilities are gated: privileged gateway intents (Presence, Server Members, Message Content) require approval for verified apps in 100 or more servers, and verification is required for apps in 100+ servers.

api_surface.breadth: Broad public API with HTTP endpoints across app, guild, channel, message, interaction, webhook, and application features, plus a separate realtime Gateway event surface documented alongside the REST API.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Discord MCP server or official MCP documentation was verified in the gathered evidence.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Discord documents per-route and global rate limits, returns X-RateLimit-* headers, and instructs clients to honor Retry-After/429 responses. Rate limits may share buckets across similar endpoints and can differ for emoji routes/top-level resources.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs highlight REST plus Gateway architecture, OAuth2 and bot auth, privileged intents, interactions/slash-command app model, and webhook-related application features. The developer policy page is active and Discord maintains a dedicated developer docs site.
raw_result.human_review_reason: MCP existence could not be confirmed beyond absence in gathered official evidence, and API surface classification is somewhat ambiguous because Discord exposes both REST and a separate Gateway websocket surface while the schema only allows REST/GraphQL/Both/None.
